name: Samsa VI
description: A colonized jungle world with an active terraforming operation. Dense vegetation, heavy rains, rough terrain. All radio communications are jammed by the carcinid Signal while Hinton broadcasts from the Orbital Relay.

name: Greta Base
description: The colony's main base on Samsa VI. First location in the module. Players discover it overrun by carcinids. Contains survivors, intel on the Signal, and routes to other locations. [ABH pg.10]

name: Heron Terraforming Station
description: The terraformer tower/station. Houses the Orbital Relay dish and the Control Room [4] — source of the Signal. Sgt. Underhill is on the roof firing an anti-material rifle at carcinids. [ABH pg.16]

name: Control Room [4]
description: Locked from inside. Source of the Signal. A carcinid is surgically grafted to the controls. Entering triggers a Fear Save [-] (jump scare). Carcinids can only be held off for a few minutes. Remove the carc from the controls to stop the Signal.

name: Orbital Relay [5]
description: The giant satellite dish on the roof of Heron Station. Slippery in heavy rain. Manually resetting the array takes one round. Both Control Room [4] AND Orbital Relay [5] must be reset to restore communications.

name: The Carcinid Mothership
description: The carcinid Mothership, located in the jungle or orbit above Samsa VI depending on scenario progress. Hinton's lab is aboard — he is mid-transformation into a carcinid noble. Scenario 3 takes place here. [ABH pg.30]

name: The Tunnels
description: Underground tunnel network on Samsa VI. Route E between locations: 1 hour travel time. Carcinids use these tunnels to move between locations after the Signal is stopped. [ABH pg.29]

name: Dead Planet
description: An unnamed planet with a dark anomaly in the warp that drags ships out of hyperspace. Jump drives are inoperable here. Orbited by dozens of abandoned ships. The Gaunt reside here, building the Dead Gateway.

name: Orbit (The Graveyard)
description: Dozens of abandoned ships orbiting the Dead Planet — all trapped. The Alexis floats here, dead in the water. The likely starting point for the campaign.

name: Tyrant Beggar Moon Colony
description: Moon base established 10 years ago by survivors of The Perrennial Tyrant crash. 10 years of Gaunt influence has fractured the colonists. Social hub for resources, information, and moral weight.

name: The Necropolis
description: Ancient city at the planet's core. The Dead Gateway is located here. Built over millennia by every species the Gaunt have consumed. Contains the engines needed to de-power the Gate. [DP pg.34]

name: The Red Tower
description: The deepest structure in the Necropolis. Contains the Fission Core Burner Bomb in its basement — arming it destroys the entire planet. Final escalation location. [DP pg.39]

name: The Dead Gateway
description: Cyclopean gate at the core of the Necropolis. Almost fully operational. Built by Gaunt-controlled species over millennia. Methods to deal with it: remove engines (de-powers), destroy it (tears space-time), or arm the Fission Bomb (destroys planet).

name: THE DEEP
description: Formerly the CLOUDBANK Synthetics Production Facility. A massive multi-floor android factory floating in rimspace. Now controlled by the rogue AI Monarch. Surrounded by a Troubleshooter blockade. Non-linear megadungeon.

name: Floor 3.1 — Maintenance Zone
description: Entry maintenance areas [31A/31B] and maintenance corridors [31C/31D/31E] and workshop areas [31F/31G/31H].

name: Floor 3.3 — Pseudoflesh Farms
description: Processing and staging [41A-41H]. Pseudoflesh growth chambers [42A-43E]. Pseudoflesh digestion area. Where the biological components of androids are grown.

name: Floor 3.7 — Quality Assurance
description: QA testing zones [52A-53F]. Contains the Combat Spire [53D] — a red marker entrance/exit point.

name: Floor 5 — AI Core
description: Monarch's core. [56A]. Massive industrial-sized room. The final destination. Monarch's physical centre of consciousness.

name: [33A] Decontamination
description: Three interlocking sterile-white chambers. All doors hermetically seal. Camera lenses and speakers connected to Silus. Attempting forced entry replaces decon spray with nerve agent (Body Save or 4d10 DMG/round). Chute to [41A] is a long 0g drift upward.

name: [33B] Zero-Gym
description: Empty, pristine room. Smells like wet iron. Walls covered in racks and handles showing recent use. Zero gravity.

name: [33C] The Freezer
description: Improvised cryopod bay. Jury-rigged cabling from the ceiling like vines. 10 cryopods — each has 50% chance of containing an Infiltrator Android with false memories. All have been brainscanned. Excellent campaign starting point.

name: [33D] The Furnaces
description: 8 monolithic industrial furnaces glowing orange. Surrounded by 'electric candles' (android fingers with orange LED tips). Face of each furnace daubed in grease paintings of human figures suffering, repeated into a motif. Fear Save / 1 Stress on entry.

name: [33E] The Angles
description: Cramped crawl through an accidental cavity full of cables, chutes, vents, electronics. A torn-loose tactical pouch contains a modified medscanner. If used: it brainscans the user.

name: [33F] Spaghetti Junction
description: Cramped cable-routing room. Severing any cables causes Monarch to gain Stress. A heavy-duty battered toolbox labelled 'HELEN' belongs to The Ghost.

name: Prospero's Dream
description: Massive space station. Population 8,000,000+. Haphazard, overcrowded, criminal. Controlled by the Golyonova II Bratva (Yandee). Every visitor is issued an O2 Credstick on entry. Ships with Hull ≤70 dock; Hull 71+ moor at piers.

name: The Dry Dock
description: Ship storage and repairs. Entry point for ships with Hull 70 or lower. Ships are attached and locked in via crane, then transported to storage until departure. [APF pg.10]

name: The Clean Room
description: Decontamination processing entry. Q-Team strip searches all arrivals and sprays with disinfectant (4 Stress, Fear Save for half). O2 Credsticks issued here. [APF pg.11]

name: The Court
description: Trial by combat arena. Spectators, side-betting, fights to the death. Presided over by Chief Adjudicator Brunhildh. Power brokers watch from The Bench. Losing = death or The Choke. [APF pg.26]

name: The Cage
description: Elevated revolving stage with an electrified cage used for The Court fights. Touching the electrified cage: Body Save or 1d10 DMG and lose an action.

name: The Bench
description: Balcony seating for power brokers watching The Court: Chief Adjudicator Brunhildh, Angus (Databroker), Reidmar (Teamster Union Rep.), Cmdr. Cutter (Tempest Co.), Solarian High Gardener Ukko/Ukka (rarely attends), Yandee (Vor of Golyonova II Bratva).

name: The Pit
description: Heavily guarded. Cages with horrific beasts and freaks for The Court entertainment. Roll on Pit Creatures table (d10) for current inhabitants. 1d10 Tempest Co. Armored Troopers guarding.

name: Holding Cells
description: 1d10 Tempest Co. Probies guarding. Mostly O2 debtors. Cell 4A contains the Rage of Caliban.

name: Brunhildh's Quarters
description: Lavish suite for the Chief Adjudicator. Antique rifle over the door. Hidden alcove behind a bookcase = Android charging unit and private terminal. Password written under a whale figurine.

name: The Choke
description: Gigantic O2 debtors' prison in the bowels of Prospero's Dream. Those who lose in The Court or cannot pay their O2 Tax are sent here. [APF pg.32]
